message: writeIfAbsent does not remove the file it just created if WriteString fails. Because os.OpenFile with O_CREATE|O_EXCL has already created the (now empty or partially written) file on disk before the write error, a subsequent re-run of `debate init`/`debate new` sees the file as existing and reports 'skipped (already exists)' rather than retrying — leaving a corrupt persona that config.Load / persona.ParseFile will reject (empty body or truncated frontmatter), making the workspace unloadable until the user manually deletes the stray file.
trigger: An I/O error during WriteString after the file is created (e.g., disk full or quota exceeded mid-write), followed by a re-run of the same command.
fix_direction: On WriteString error, close and os.Remove(path) before returning the error so a re-run can recreate the file cleanly (or write to a temp file and rename into place).
uncertainty: The failure requires an I/O error to land specifically between the successful O_EXCL create and a completed write — a low-probability environment condition rather than an input-driven path. Severity and likelihood are low; reported as candidate.
evidence: writeIfAbsent (scaffold.go:176-187) opens with os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL|os.O_WRONLY, then `_, err = f.WriteString(content); return err == nil, err`. There is no os.Remove(path) on the write-error path. On re-run, the EXCL open returns os.ErrExist, which the function maps to (false, nil), so cmdInit prints 'skipped ... (already exists)' (scaffold.go:82) and never recreates the file. persona.parse rejects an empty body ('system prompt body is empty', persona.go:77).

message: TestCmdNew_RejectsPathSeparators does not actually verify path-separator/name validation. It runs in a bare t.TempDir() with no .heurema/debate workspace, so config.Discover fails for every input. Because validatePersonaName (scaffold.go:117) runs before config.Discover (scaffold.go:127) but the test only asserts code != 0 and never inspects stderr, the non-zero exit is guaranteed by the missing-workspace path regardless of name validation. The test would still pass if validatePersonaName were a no-op, giving false confidence in the path-traversal protection.
fix_direction: Run the test inside an initialized workspace (call cmdInit first, like the other cmdNew tests) so a valid name would otherwise succeed, and assert the error message identifies the validation cause (e.g. stderr contains 'letters, digits' or 'must contain only'). This isolates name validation from the missing-workspace path.
evidence: In cmdNew (scaffold.go:116-131), validatePersonaName is checked first, then config.Discover. The test workDir has no .heurema/debate, so Discover returns an error for any name. The assertion is only `if code == 0 { t.Errorf(...) }` with no stderr check, unlike TestCmdNew_RequiresWorkspace (line 188) and TestCmdNew_InvalidRole (line 200, which runs in an initialized workspace so only the role check can fail).

message: No test asserts that `debate init` creates context.md. config.Load swallows a missing context.md (os.ErrNotExist is treated as empty context, config.go:90-93), so TestCmdInit_CreatesWorkspaceThatLoads passing does not prove context.md exists. The acceptance criterion requires init to scaffold a context.md template, but its creation is unverified.
fix_direction: Add an assertion that .heurema/debate/context.md exists (os.Stat) and optionally that its contents match contextTemplate after cmdInit.
evidence: cmdInit writes context.md (scaffold.go:70), but TestCmdInit_StarterPersonasParseable only checks proposer.md and skeptic.md, and TestCmdInit_CreatesWorkspaceThatLoads relies on config.Load, which does not require context.md. No os.Stat/ReadFile assertion targets .heurema/debate/context.md.

message: The 'too many positional arguments' error path in cmdNew (scaffold.go:110-114) has no test. cmdNew explicitly handles fs.NArg() > 1 with a distinct error and exit code, but no test passes more than one positional argument.
fix_direction: Add a test invoking cmdNew with two positional arguments and assert a non-zero exit (optionally that stderr mentions the one-argument requirement).
evidence: scaffold.go:110-114 returns 1 with 'takes exactly one positional argument' when NArg() > 1. Test functions cover missing name (TestCmdNew_MissingName) but none invoke cmdNew with two positional args, e.g. cmdNew([]string{"a", "b"}, ...).

message: outcomeString is a no-op: every switch branch (including default) returns the input reason unchanged, so the function is an identity wrapper with a meaningless switch. It adds indirection without behavior.
fix_direction: Either inline the call to use result.Outcome.Reason directly and delete outcomeString, or, if a normalization/whitelist was intended, implement the actual mapping (e.g. return a fixed string for unknown reasons) instead of returning reason in every branch.
evidence: func outcomeString(reason string) string { switch reason { case "settled", "stalemate", "max": return reason; default: return reason } } — all paths return reason. Called at line 182 (Outcome: outcomeString(result.Outcome.Reason)).

message: docs/DESIGN.md §7 and docs/SLICES.md §7 state that `debate init` scaffolds a `config.yml`, but the implemented init creates only personas/proposer.md, personas/skeptic.md, and context.md (no config.yml). The user-facing CLI reference now misdescribes what `init` produces.
trigger: A user reads docs/DESIGN.md §7 or docs/SLICES.md §7 to learn what `debate init` creates and expects a config.yml that is never written.
fix_direction: Update DESIGN.md:306 and SLICES.md:72 to state that init scaffolds two debater personas plus context.md and relies on the built-in default synthesizer (no config.yml is written).
evidence: scaffold.go:64-71 writes only proposer.md, skeptic.md, context.md. DESIGN.md:306 'debate init  скаффолд .heurema/debate/ (config + стартовые персоны)'; SLICES.md:72 'config.yml + context.md + стартовые proposer.md/skeptic.md'. Contract assumption explicitly: init does not scaffold a config.yml because the default panel is all debater personas.

message: docs/DESIGN.md:307 documents `debate new <name> [--tags ...]`, but the implemented subcommand exposes `--role debater|synthesizer` and has no `--tags` flag. The actually-supported `--role` flag is undocumented in the CLI reference.
trigger: A user reads docs/DESIGN.md §7 to discover flags for `debate new` and either tries the non-existent `--tags` or misses the real `--role` flag.
fix_direction: Document the implemented `--role debater|synthesizer` flag in DESIGN.md:307; keep `--tags` only if it is clearly marked as a future/not-yet-available feature.
uncertainty: DESIGN.md is partly forward-looking and tags are an explicitly deferred v2 feature, so the `--tags` entry may be an intentional placeholder; unclear whether the design doc is expected to be reconciled to the implemented `--role` flag as part of this slice.
evidence: scaffold.go:95 `fs.StringVar(&role, "role", "debater", "persona role (debater|synthesizer)")` and usage line 97 `usage: debate new [--role debater|synthesizer] <name>`. DESIGN.md:307 shows `[--tags ...]`; tags are a deferred v2 feature per SLICES.md:83 and an open question per DESIGN.md:367.
